question_text: A wooden block floats in water, but an iron block of the same volume sinks. What does this tell us about their densities?

options.label: A
options.text: Wood is denser than water, iron is less dense than water

options.label: B
options.text: Wood and iron have the same density

options.label: C
options.text: Wood is less dense than water, iron is denser than water

options.label: D
options.text: Density has no effect on whether objects float or sink

correct_answer: Wood is less dense than water, iron is denser than water
explanation: Objects float when they are less dense than the liquid they are in, and sink when they are denser. Since wood floats and iron sinks in water, wood must be less dense than water and iron must be denser than water.
